---
title: 'run-cached'
---

Run a build with a `ParquetCache` wrapping the expression. Identical to
[`xorq run`](run.qmd) in semantics, but the resulting expression is wrapped in a
parquet cache so subsequent invocations can short-circuit when inputs haven't
changed.

## Usage

```bash
xorq run-cached <build_path> [OPTIONS]
```

## Arguments

- **`build_path`** — Path to the build directory produced by `xorq build`.

## Options

| Option | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-o`, `--output-path` | `/dev/null` (discard) | Path to write output. Use `-` for stdout. |
| `-f`, `--format` | `parquet` | Output format. One of `csv`, `json`, `parquet`. |
| `--cache-dir` | system cache dir | Directory for parquet cache files. |
| `--limit` | unlimited | Maximum number of rows to output. |
| `--cache-type` | `modification-time` | Cache strategy. One of `modification-time` (`ParquetCache`) or `snapshot` (`ParquetSnapshotCache`). |
| `--ttl` | none | TTL in seconds for snapshot cache. When set, uses `ParquetTTLSnapshotCache` instead. |
| `-p`, `--params` | none | Override expression parameters. Format: `key=value`. Repeatable. |

## Cache strategies

- **`modification-time`** (default) — `ParquetCache`. Inputs are tracked by file
  modification time; the cache is invalidated when an input file's mtime changes.
- **`snapshot`** — `ParquetSnapshotCache`. The cache is keyed by a content
  snapshot of the inputs and never invalidates implicitly.
- **`snapshot` + `--ttl`** — `ParquetTTLSnapshotCache`. As above, but the cache
  entry expires after the supplied TTL (in seconds).

## Examples

Default behavior (modification-time cache):
```bash
xorq run-cached builds/f02d28198715 --cache-dir ./cache -o results.parquet
```

Snapshot cache with a 1-hour TTL:
```bash
xorq run-cached builds/f02d28198715 \
    --cache-type snapshot \
    --ttl 3600 \
    --cache-dir ./cache \
    -o results.parquet
```

With parameter overrides:
```bash
xorq run-cached builds/f02d28198715 \
    -p threshold=0.5 \
    --cache-dir ./cache \
    -o results.parquet
```
